judgment.summary: Deere & Company shows a blend of strong profitability and returns offset by recent revenue declines and high leverage.
judgment.earningsRecap: For the quarter ending 2026-01-31, Deere & Company reported an EPS of $2.42, beating the estimate of $2.10 by 15.4%. The next earnings date is scheduled for 2026-05-21.
positives: Profit margin at 10.3% and operating margin at 17.5% indicate strong operational efficiency.
Return on equity of 19.6% demonstrates effective use of shareholder investments.
Forward P/E of 24.25x suggests a more attractive valuation relative to future earnings expectations compared to the trailing P/E of 31.44x.
The current ratio of 2.25 and quick ratio of 1.99 indicate a healthy short-term liquidity position.
Analyst consensus is a 'buy' with a mean target price of $665.10, suggesting upside potential.
negatives: Revenue growth of -11.1% and earnings growth of -8.5% year-over-year indicate a recent contraction in business.
Analyst growth estimates for the current quarter (-14.2%) and current fiscal year (-3.4%) point to continued expected declines.
The Debt/Equity ratio is very high at 376.02, signaling significant financial leverage.
Total debt of $48.47B is substantial relative to total cash of $8.83B.
The PEG ratio of 1.70 suggests that the stock price may be high relative to its expected earnings growth.

news.paragraph: Deere & Company is approaching its Q2 earnings report, a significant catalyst that traders will be watching closely to gauge expected stock movement. While the company is also facing a new right-to-repair lawsuit concerning its construction equipment, recent headlines also highlight analyst target price discussions and comparisons to competitors like Caterpillar. The broader market sentiment appears cautious, influenced by global economic factors and upcoming earnings from other major companies.
